question_text: Why does the UK have longer daylight hours in summer than in winter?

options.label: A
options.text: The UK is closer to the Sun in summer

options.label: B
options.text: Earth's tilted axis means the UK is tilted towards the Sun in summer

options.label: C
options.text: The Sun moves faster across the sky in summer

options.label: D
options.text: Earth orbits the Sun faster in summer

explanation: Earth's axis is tilted 23.5°. In summer (June), the UK is tilted towards the Sun, so the Sun is higher in the sky and above the horizon for longer, creating longer daylight hours.
